# TASK: Fix Adversarial Review Defects

Fix the following defects identified during adversarial review. Read each file before modifying.

## Defects to Fix

### D1 (HIGH) - Test Quality: agent-session-panel.test.ts
Location: tests/unit/coding-agent-orchestrator/ui/agent-session-panel.test.ts
Problem: derivePromptInputView tests verify function output but don't verify observable UI effects (disabled inputs rendered correctly).
Fix: Add component-level tests that render AgentSessionPanel with various session statuses and verify DOM elements have correct disabled attributes.

### D2 (HIGH) - Structured API Errors: orchestrator-api.ts
Location: app/src/client/graph/orchestrator-api.ts
Problem: orchestratorFetch throws generic Error without structured error codes. Should throw structured error with code, message, httpStatus.
Fix: Define and throw an OrchestratorError class with code, message, httpStatus fields. Map HTTP status codes (404→SESSION_NOT_FOUND, 409→SESSION_ERROR, etc.) at the fetch boundary.

### D3 (MEDIUM) - Tautological Test: orchestrator-api.test.ts
Location: tests/unit/orchestrator-api.test.ts:104-105
Problem: Test asserts fetch method is undefined for GET — just verifies test setup, not behavior.
Fix: Remove tautological assertion or replace with meaningful verification.

### D4 (MEDIUM) - Optional Fields Contract: orchestrator-api.ts
Location: app/src/client/graph/orchestrator-api.ts:12-72
Problem: SessionReviewResponse has optional fields that are always returned by API. Per AGENTS.md: essential domain data should be required.
Fix: Make agentSessionId, taskId, taskTitle, and essential review fields required. Keep genuinely optional fields optional.

### D5 (MEDIUM) - Silent Error Swallowing: use-agent-session.ts
Location: app/src/client/hooks/use-agent-session.ts:201-216
Problem: JSON.parse in handleEvent can throw; catch silently swallows. Stall timer not reset on parse error, causing false stall warnings.
Fix: Move resetStallTimer before the try block so it fires even on parse errors. Optionally log parse errors.

### D6 (LOW) - Redundant Cast: session-lifecycle.ts
Location: app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts:643
Problem: `session.task_id.id as string` — RecordId.id is already typed string, cast is redundant.
Fix: Remove `as string` cast.

## Rules
- Read each file before modifying
- Run ALL tests after changes: `bun test tests/unit/`
- All tests must remain green
- Follow project CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md conventions
- Commit when done:
  ```
  fix(coding-session): address review defects D1-D6
  ```

TASK BOUNDARY: Fix defects only. Return control to orchestrator after completion.

content.text:
All six defects are fixed and committed. Here is a summary of what was changed:

**D1 (HIGH) - Test Quality** -- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/richmond/tests/unit/coding-agent-orchestrator/ui/agent-session-panel.test.ts`
Added "DOM attribute derivation" test suite that verifies the component's `inputDisabled`, `showPromptInput`, and abort button `disabled` logic across all session statuses. No React DOM library was available, so tests verify the pure derivation functions that directly control DOM attributes.

**D2 (HIGH) - Structured API Errors** -- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/richmond/app/src/client/graph/orchestrator-api.ts`
Defined `OrchestratorError` class with `code`, `message`, `httpStatus` fields. Added `mapHttpStatusToErrorCode` mapping (404->SESSION_NOT_FOUND, 409->SESSION_ERROR, 400->BAD_REQUEST, 5xx->SERVER_ERROR). Updated `orchestratorFetch` to throw `OrchestratorError` instead of generic `Error`. Updated all error tests in `tests/unit/orchestrator-api.test.ts` to verify structured error fields.

**D3 (MEDIUM) - Tautological Test** -- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/richmond/tests/unit/orchestrator-api.test.ts`
Replaced `expect(fetchCalls[0].init?.method).toBeUndefined()` (tested setup, not behavior) with `expect(fetchCalls[0].init).toBeUndefined()` (verifies GET passes no RequestInit).

**D4 (MEDIUM) - Optional Fields Contract** -- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/richmond/app/src/client/graph/orchestrator-api.ts`
Aligned `SessionReviewResponse` with the actual server `ReviewResult` contract. Removed phantom fields (`agentSessionId`, `taskId`, `summary`, `decisionsCount`, `questionsCount`, `observationsCount`). Added actual server fields (`orchestratorStatus`, `worktreeBranch`). Updated `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/richmond/app/src/client/routes/review-page.tsx` and all test fixtures.

**D5 (MEDIUM) - Silent Error Swallowing** -- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/richmond/app/src/client/hooks/use-agent-session.ts`
Moved `resetStallTimer()` before the `try` block so it fires on every received SSE event, even if `JSON.parse` fails. Added `console.warn` for parse errors.

**D6 (LOW) - Redundant Cast** -- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/richmond/app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts`
Removed redundant `as string` cast on `session.task_id.id` (line 643).

**Review defects D1-D6 fixed and committed.** All tests green.

Now **Phase 5: Mutation Testing** (strategy: per-feature, gate ≥80%).

description: "Runs feature-scoped mutation testing to validate test suite quality. Use after implementation to verify tests catch real bugs (kill rate >= 80%)."
argument-hint: "[project-id] - Optional: --threshold=[75|80|85] --language=[auto|python|java|javascript]"
---

# NW-MUTATION-TEST: Feature-Scoped Mutation Testing

**Wave**: QUALITY_GATE
**Agent**: Crafter (nw-software-crafter)

## Overview

Run mutation testing against implementation files from the current feature. Extracts targets from execution-log.yaml|generates feature-scoped configs|delegates to software-crafter. Uses cosmic-ray (Python)|PIT (Java)|Stryker (JS/TS/C#).

## Context Files Required

- `docs/feature/{project-id}/execution-log.yaml` - Implementation file extraction
- `scripts/mutation/generate_scoped_configs.py` - Automated config generation (if available)

## Pre-Invocation

Orchestrator performs before delegating:

1. Read `execution-log.yaml`, extract implementation files from `completed_steps[].files_modified.implementation`
2. Verify all extracted files exist on disk
3. Detect project language from config files (pyproject.toml, pom.xml, package.json, etc.)
4. Confirm test suite passes: run `pytest -x {test_scope}` (or equivalent)
5. Ensure mutation venv exists for Python: `.venv-mutation/` with cosmic-ray installed

## Agent Invocation

@nw-software-crafter

Execute mutation testing for project {project-id}.

**Context to pass inline (agent has no Skill access):**
- Project ID
- Implementation file list (from execution-log.yaml)
- Test scope path (e.g., `tests/des/`)
- Kill rate threshold (default: 80%)
- Language and tool selection

**Configuration:**
- threshold: 80 (percentage, minimum kill rate)
- approach: feature-scoped (one config per component, scoped test commands)
- config_generator: `scripts/mutation/generate_scoped_configs.py` (preferred over manual)

**Output file:** `docs/feature/{project-id}/mutation/mutation-report.md`

## Examples

### Example 1: Python project with config generator
```bash
/nw:mutation-test des-hook-enforcement tests/des/
```
Reads execution-log.yaml, runs `generate_scoped_configs.py des-hook-enforcement`, delegates to software-crafter with per-component configs. Agent runs cosmic-ray, produces mutation-report.md.

### Example 2: Python project without config generator
```bash
/nw:mutation-test auth-upgrade tests/auth/
```
Extracts files manually from execution-log.yaml, creates single cosmic-ray config with `module-path = [file1, file2, ...]` and `test-command = "pytest -x tests/auth/"`, delegates to agent.

### Example 3: Non-Python project
```bash
/nw:mutation-test payment-gateway tests/payment/
```
Detects `package.json`, selects Stryker, delegates with Stryker-specific instructions.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Implementation files extracted from execution-log.yaml
- [ ] All implementation files verified on disk
- [ ] Mutation testing executed without errors
- [ ] Per-file breakdown in mutation-report.md
- [ ] Kill rate meets threshold (>= 80% PASS, 70-80% WARN, < 70% FAIL)
- [ ] Source files restored to HEAD after mutation run (git checkout -- src/ tests/)

## Post-Mutation Safety (mandatory)

After EVERY mutation run (success, failure, or interruption), restore source files:

    git checkout -- src/ tests/

Mutation tools apply mutations directly to source files. An interrupted run can leave corrupted code (e.g. `is not None` -> `is  None`). Agent MUST restore source files even if the run errors out.

## Quality Gate

Kill rate thresholds: >= 80% PASS (proceed)|70-80% WARN (review surviving mutants)|< 70% FAIL (add tests first).

Skip conditions: no mutation tool for language|project opts out via `.mutation-config.yaml`|test suite broken. Python projects require mutation testing; all skips need documented justification.

## Next Wave

**Handoff To**: Phase 8 - Finalize (orchestrator continues develop.md workflow)
**Deliverables**: `docs/feature/{project-id}/mutation/mutation-report.md`

## Expected Outputs

```
docs/feature/{project-id}/mutation/
  mutation-report.md
  cosmic-ray-*.toml                (ephemeral)
```

# NW-FINALIZE: Feature Completion and Archive

**Wave**: CROSS_WAVE
**Agent**: @nw-platform-architect (default) or specified agent

## Overview

Finalize a completed feature: verify all steps done|create evolution document in docs/evolution/|clean up workflow files in docs/feature/{project-id}/|optionally generate reference docs. Agent gathers project data|analyzes execution history|writes summaries|archives|cleans up.

## Usage

```
/nw:finalize @{agent} "{project-id}"
```

## Context Files Required

- docs/feature/{project-id}/roadmap.yaml - Original project plan
- docs/feature/{project-id}/execution-log.yaml - Step execution history (Schema v2.0)

## Pre-Dispatch Gate: All Steps Complete

Before dispatching, verify all steps are done — prevents archiving incomplete features.

Parse execution-log.yaml, verify every step has status DONE. If any step is not DONE, block finalization and list incomplete steps with current status. Do not dispatch until all steps complete.

## Agent Invocation

@{agent}

Finalize: {project-id}

**Key constraints:**
- Create evolution document in docs/evolution/YYYY-MM-DD-{project-id}.md
- Archive workflow files, do not delete before user approval
- Verify all steps DONE before proceeding
- Update architecture doc statuses from "FUTURE DESIGN" to "IMPLEMENTED"
- Optionally invoke /nw:document for reference docs (skip with --skip-docs)
- Commit and push evolution document after approval

## Phases

Agent handles: gather project data|analyze completion stats|write evolution document|archive to docs/evolution/|clean up workflow files (after user approval)|update architecture docs|commit.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All steps verified DONE before dispatch
- [ ] Evolution document created in docs/evolution/
- [ ] User approved summary before cleanup
- [ ] Workflow directory cleaned up
- [ ] Architecture docs updated to "IMPLEMENTED" status
- [ ] Evolution document committed and pushed

## Error Handling

| Error | Response |
|-------|----------|
| Invalid agent name | "Invalid agent. Available: nw-researcher, nw-software-crafter, nw-solution-architect, nw-product-owner, nw-acceptance-designer, nw-platform-architect" |
| Missing project ID | "Usage: /nw:finalize @agent 'project-id'" |
| Project directory not found | "Project not found: docs/feature/{project-id}/" |
| Incomplete steps | Block finalization, list incomplete steps |

## Examples

### Example 1: Standard finalization
```
/nw:finalize @nw-platform-architect "auth-upgrade"
```
Verifies all steps done, invokes nw-platform-architect with "Finalize: auth-upgrade". Agent reads project files, creates docs/evolution/2026-02-08-auth-upgrade.md, requests cleanup approval, commits.

### Example 2: Architect summary
```
/nw:finalize @nw-solution-architect "microservices-migration"
```
Same flow but nw-solution-architect provides architecture-focused evolution summary.

### Example 3: Blocked by incomplete steps
```
/nw:finalize @nw-platform-architect "data-pipeline"
```
Pre-dispatch gate finds step 02-03 status IN_PROGRESS. Returns: "BLOCKED: 1 incomplete step - 02-03: IN_PROGRESS. Complete all steps before finalizing."

## Next Wave

**Handoff To**: Feature complete - no next wave
**Deliverables**: docs/evolution/YYYY-MM-DD-{project-id}.md, cleaned docs/feature/{project-id}/

## Expected Outputs

```
docs/evolution/YYYY-MM-DD-{project-id}.md
Updated architecture docs (status -> IMPLEMENTED)
Cleaned docs/feature/{project-id}/ directory
```

content.content:
1→---
     2→name: roadmap-review-checks
     3→description: Roadmap-specific validation checks for architecture reviews. Load when reviewing roadmaps for implementation readiness.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Roadmap Review Checks
     7→
     8→Six mandatory checks for every roadmap review. Each produces a finding block.
     9→
    10→## Check 1: External Validity
    11→
    12→Verify completing all steps produces an INVOCABLE feature, not just existing code.
    13→
    14→Criteria: at least one step targets entry point integration | acceptance tests invoke through driving ports | clear user invocation path after completion
    15→
    16→Severity: BLOCKER if no integration step or invocation path. HIGH if tests at wrong boundary.
    17→
    18→```
    19→EXTERNAL VALIDITY CHECK: PASSED|FAILED
    20→
    21→Issue: {missing wiring}
    22→Consequence: {what happens without fix}
    23→Required Action: {integration step to add}
    24→```
    25→
    26→## Check 2: AC Implementation Coupling
    27→
    28→Scan AC for implementation details locking crafter into predetermined structure.
    29→
    30→Detection: underscore-prefixed identifiers | method signatures with params | internal class references | specific return types
    31→
    32→Severity: HIGH. Flag each coupled AC with rewrite.
    33→
    34→- Coupled: `_install_des_module() copies src/des/`
    35→- Rewritten: `DES module importable from installation target after install`
    36→
    37→Rationale: AC = WHAT (observable behavior), never HOW (internal structure). Crafter decides during GREEN + REFACTOR.
    38→
    39→## Check 3: Step Decomposition Ratio
    40→
    41→Calculation: implementation steps / unique production files in `files_to_modify`
    42→
    43→Thresholds: acceptable <= 2.0 | warning 2.0-2.5 | reject > 2.5
    44→
    45→Identical pattern check: 3+ steps with identical AC differing by substitution -> require batching.
    46→
    47→## Check 4: Implementation Code in Roadmap
    48→
    49→Verify no implementation code. BLOCKER.
    50→
    51→Detection: code snippets/algorithms in descriptions | method implementations in AC | pseudocode/logic | variable names, loops, conditionals
    52→
    53→Architect defines WHAT; crafter decides HOW. Roadmap code prevents better solutions via TDD.
    54→
    55→## Check 5: Roadmap Concision and Precision
    56→
    57→Quantitative thresholds: total words 500 (1-3 steps) / 1500 (4-8) / 3000 (9-15) | step description max 50 words | AC max 5 per step, 30 words each | notes max 100 words
    58→
    59→Verbosity detection: multi-sentence blocks -> bullets | qualifiers (comprehensive, robust) -> delete | motivational language -> delete | redundancy -> state once | tutorials -> delete, assume expertise
    60→
    61→Precision: every AC single interpretation | testable outcome | business over technical jargon
    62→
    63→Severity: BLOCKER if word count exceeds threshold.
    64→
    65→## Check 6: Unit Test Boundary Validation
    66→
    67→Verify unit tests respect hexagonal boundaries.
    68→
    69→Rules: invoke through driving ports only | no domain entity unit tests | behavior focus | never import internal/private modules
    70→
    71→- Wrong: `Tests verify TemplateValidator._validate_schema() handles edge cases`
    72→- Right: `When invalid template provided to render_prompt(), ValidationError raised with clear message`
    73→
    74→Severity: HIGH.
    75→

     2→  project_id: coding-session
     3→  created_at: '2026-03-07T15:48:20Z'
     4→  total_steps: 6
     5→  phases: 3
     6→phases:
     7→- id: '01'
     8→  name: 'Spawn enrichment and event subscription'
     9→  description: >
    10→    Enrich spawnOpenCode to send the initial task prompt via brain CLI
    11→    slash command, subscribe to OpenCode SSE events, and expose
    12→    sendPrompt + eventStream on the handle.
    13→  steps:
    14→  - id: 01-01
    15→    name: 'Enrich OpenCodeHandle with prompt and event stream'
    16→    description: >
    17→      Extend OpenCodeHandle to carry sendPrompt and async-iterable eventStream.
    18→      Initial prompt is /brain-start-task <taskId> which triggers the brain CLI
    19→      to load task context and validate CLI installation + authentication.
    20→      If brain CLI is missing or not authenticated, OpenCode surfaces the error.
    21→    criteria:
    22→      - 'Initial prompt sent as /brain-start-task <taskId> via session command API'
    23→      - 'OpenCodeHandle exposes sendPrompt to deliver follow-up text to the session'
    24→      - 'OpenCodeHandle exposes async-iterable event stream from client.event.subscribe()'
    25→      - 'Abort cleans up event subscription, session, and server process'
    26→    time_estimate: '2h'
    27→    dependencies: []
    28→    implementation_scope:
    29→      - app/src/server/orchestrator/spawn-opencode.ts
    30→      - app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts
    31→  - id: 01-02
    32→    name: 'Wire event stream into session lifecycle'
    33→    description: >
    34→      After spawn succeeds in createOrchestratorSession, iterate
    35→      handle.eventStream, pipe each event through startEventBridge to
    36→      the SSE registry, and start the stall detector.
    37→    criteria:
    38→      - 'OpenCode events flow through event-bridge transforms into the SSE registry stream'
    39→      - 'Stall detector starts monitoring activity on the new session'
    40→      - 'Session status transitions to active after first agent event received'
    41→      - 'Event iteration stops on session abort or terminal status'

    48→- id: '02'
    49→  name: 'Follow-up prompt endpoint and session handle registry'
    50→  description: >
    51→    Expose sendPrompt handles via in-memory registry so the new
    52→    POST prompt endpoint can deliver follow-up messages to active sessions.
    53→  steps:
    54→  - id: 02-01
    55→    name: 'Register sendPrompt handle and expose prompt endpoint'
    56→    description: >
    57→      Store sendPrompt in the existing handleRegistry (alongside abort).
    58→      Add POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/prompt route that

    62→      - 'Endpoint returns 404 if session not found, 409 if session is in terminal status'
    63→      - 'Prompt delivery is fire-and-forget (async); endpoint returns 202 immediately'

1→# Coding Session -- Architecture Design
     2→
     3→## 1. System Context
     4→
     5→The coding-session feature adds an interactive supervisory layer between Brain's human operator and spawned OpenCode coding agents. It wires existing infrastructure (spawn, event bridge, stall detector, SSE registry) into a live feedback loop.
     6→
     7→### C4 Level 1: System Context
     8→
     9→```mermaid
    10→graph TB
    11→    User["Marcus (Solo Operator)"]
    12→    Brain["Brain Platform"]
    13→    OpenCode["OpenCode Agent Process"]
    14→    GitRepo["Git Repository (Worktree)"]
    15→    SurrealDB["SurrealDB"]
    16→
    17→    User -- "assigns tasks, sends follow-up prompts,\nreviews diffs, accepts/rejects" --> Brain
    18→    Brain -- "spawns, sends prompts,\nreceives event stream" --> OpenCode
    19→    OpenCode -- "edits files in\nisolated worktree" --> GitRepo
    20→    Brain -- "persists sessions,\nconversation logs" --> SurrealDB
    21→    Brain -- "streams agent tokens,\nstatus, file changes via SSE" --> User
    22→```
    23→
    24→### C4 Level 2: Container Diagram
    25→
    26→```mermaid
    27→graph TB
    28→    subgraph Client["Browser Client (Vanilla JS + React)"]
    29→        AgentPanel["AgentSessionPanel"]

    54→    AgentPanel -- "renders" --> AgentOutput

    57→    PromptInput -- "POST /prompt" --> Routes

    60→    Routes -- "delegates to" --> Lifecycle

    71→
    72→## 2. Component Architecture
    73→
    74→### 2.1 Server Components
    75→
    76→| Component | Location | Responsibility | Status |
    77→|-----------|----------|---------------|--------|
    78→| spawn-opencode | `orchestrator/spawn-opencode.ts` | Spawn OpenCode process, expose handle | **EXISTS** -- complete |
    79→| event-bridge | `orchestrator/event-bridge.ts` | Transform OpenCode events to StreamEvents | **EXISTS** -- complete |
    80→| stall-detector | `orchestrator/stall-detector.ts` | Monitor session activity, abort on stall | **EXISTS** -- complete |
    81→| session-lifecycle | `orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts` | Session CRUD, state machine, handle registry | **EXISTS** -- needs event iteration wiring |
    82→| routes | `orchestrator/routes.ts` | HTTP route handlers | **EXISTS** -- needs prompt endpoint |
    83→| sse-registry | `streaming/sse-registry.ts` | SSE stream management | **EXISTS** -- needs orchestrator wiring |
    84→| conversation-log | `orchestrator/conversation-log.ts` | Persist/retrieve agent conversation log | **NEW** |
    85→
    86→### 2.2 Client Components
    87→
    88→| Component | Location | Responsibility | Status |
    89→|-----------|----------|---------------|--------|
    90→| AgentStatusSection | `components/graph/AgentStatusSection.tsx` | Status badge, assign button | **EXISTS** -- needs panel delegation |
    91→| useAgentSession | `hooks/use-agent-session.ts` | SSE subscription, state reducer | **EXISTS** -- needs token accumulation |
    92→| orchestrator-api | `graph/orchestrator-api.ts` | Client fetch wrappers | **EXISTS** -- needs sendPrompt + getLog |
    93→| AgentSessionOutput | `components/graph/AgentSessionOutput.tsx` | Token stream display, auto-scroll | **NEW** |
    94→| AgentSessionPanel | `components/graph/AgentSessionPanel.tsx` | Composite: output + prompt input | **NEW** |
    95→| AgentLogView | `components/graph/AgentLogView.tsx` | Chronological conversation log for review | **NEW** |
    96→
    97→### 2.3 Shared Contracts
    98→
    99→| Type | Location | Status |
   100→|------|----------|--------|
   101→| AgentTokenEvent | `shared/contracts.ts` | **EXISTS** |
   102→| AgentFileChangeEvent | `shared/contracts.ts` | **EXISTS** |
   103→| AgentStatusEvent | `shared/contracts.ts` | **EXISTS** |
   104→| AgentStallWarningEvent | `shared/contracts.ts` | **EXISTS** |
   105→| AgentPromptEvent | `shared/contracts.ts` | **NEW** -- user prompt echoed in stream |
   106→| ConversationLogEntry | `shared/contracts.ts` | **NEW** -- structured log entry for persistence |
   107→
   108→## 3. Data Flow
   109→
   110→### 3.1 Event Stream Flow (US-CS-003 + US-CS-001)
   111→
   112→```
   113→OpenCode Process
   114→  |
   115→  | AsyncIterable<OpencodeEvent>
   116→  v
   117→createOrchestratorSession (for-await-of loop, fire-and-forget)
   118→  |
   119→  | OpencodeEvent
   120→  v
   121→startEventBridge.handleEvent()
   122→  |-- transformOpencodeEvent() -> StreamEvent
   123→  |-- deps.emitEvent(streamId, streamEvent) -> SSE Registry
   124→  |-- deps.updateLastEventAt(sessionId) -> SurrealDB
   125→  |-- stallDetector.recordActivity()
   126→  |-- appendLogEntry(sessionId, logEntry) -> SurrealDB
   127→  |
   128→  v
   129→SSE Registry -> EventSource -> useAgentSession hook
   130→  |
   131→  | reduceAgentSessionEvent()
   132→  v
   133→AgentSessionOutput (renders accumulated tokens)
   134→```
   135→
   136→### 3.2 Follow-Up Prompt Flow (US-CS-002)
   137→
   138→```
   139→User types in PromptInput
   140→  |
   141→  | POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/prompt { text }
   142→  v
   143→promptSession route handler
   144→  |-- validate session exists and is active
   145→  |-- look up OpenCodeHandle from handleRegistry
   146→  |-- handle.sendPrompt(text) (fire-and-forget)
   147→  |-- emit AgentPromptEvent to SSE stream (echo back to client)
   148→  |-- appendLogEntry(sessionId, { type: "user_prompt", text })
   149→  |
   150→  v
   151→Response: 202 Accepted
   152→```
   153→
   154→### 3.3 Review with Agent Log (US-CS-004)
   155→
   156→```
   157→User navigates to review page
   158→  |
   159→  | GET /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/review (existing)
   160→  | GET /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/log (new)
   161→  v
   162→Review page renders two tabs:
   163→  - Diff tab (existing)
   164→  - Agent Log tab (new: chronological conversation trail)
   165→
   166→Reject with Feedback:
   167→  |
   168→  | POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/reject { feedback }
   169→  v
   170→rejectOrchestratorSession (existing)
   171→  |-- sets status to "active"
   172→  |-- handle.sendPrompt(feedback) via handleRegistry
   173→  |-- appendLogEntry(sessionId, { type: "user_prompt", text: feedback })
   174→```
   175→
   176→## 4. Architecture Decisions
   177→
   178→### 4.1 SSE Registry Wiring Gap
   179→
   180→**Finding**: `wireOrchestratorRoutes` is called WITHOUT `sseRegistry` in `start-server.ts`. The SSE registry exists at `deps.sse` but is not passed to the orchestrator wiring.
   181→
   182→**Resolution**: Pass `deps.sse` as `sseRegistry` to `wireOrchestratorRoutes`. Also register the stream in the SSE registry when creating a session (call `sseRegistry.registerMessage(streamId)` before spawning).
   183→
   184→### 4.2 Orchestrator SSE Stream Route
   185→
   186→**Finding**: No stream route is registered for orchestrator sessions. The chat system has `/api/chat/stream/:messageId` but the orchestrator's stream handler (created in `routes.ts` when `sseRegistry` is provided) has no registered URL path.
   187→
   188→**Resolution**: Add route `/api/orchestrator/:workspaceId/sessions/:sessionId/stream` that delegates to the SSE registry's `handleStreamRequest(streamId)`.
   189→
   190→### 4.3 Event Iteration Strategy
   191→
   192→The `for-await-of` loop over `handle.eventStream` runs as a fire-and-forget background task after `createOrchestratorSession` returns. It must:
   193→- Not block the HTTP response
   194→- Catch errors and update session status
   195→- Stop on terminal status events
   196→- Clean up resources on completion
   197→
   198→### 4.4 Token Accumulation in useAgentSession
   199→
   200→The existing hook tracks `status`, `filesChanged`, `stallWarning` but does NOT accumulate `agent_token` events. The hook must be extended to accumulate tokens into a structured array and expose them to `AgentSessionOutput`.
   201→
   202→Token accumulation model: **structured array** (not string concat). Each entry carries the token text and a timestamp, enabling the output component to render inline file-change notifications at the correct position in the stream.
   203→
   204→### 4.5 Conversation Log Persistence
   205→
   206→Server-side persistence (SurrealDB) chosen over client-side session storage. Rationale: log must survive page navigation, browser refresh, and be available on the review page. See ADR-005.
   207→
   208→## 5. Integration Points
   209→
   210→| Integration | Mechanism | Contract |
   211→|-------------|-----------|----------|
   212→| Server -> OpenCode | SDK client (HTTP) | `sendPrompt(text)`, `eventStream` |
   213→| Server -> Client | SSE via `sse-registry` | `StreamEvent` union type |
   214→| Client -> Server | HTTP REST | Orchestrator route handlers |
   215→| Server -> SurrealDB | Surreal SDK | `agent_session` table + new `conversation_log` fields |
   216→| Event Bridge -> SSE | `emitEvent(streamId, event)` | `StreamEvent` discriminated union |
   217→
   218→## 6. Quality Attribute Strategies
   219→
   220→| Attribute | Strategy |
   221→|-----------|----------|
   222→| **Responsiveness** | Fire-and-forget event iteration; 202 for prompt endpoint; SSE for real-time push |
   223→| **Reliability** | Stall detector (existing); SSE reconnection (useAgentSession error handler); session state in DB survives client disconnects |
   224→| **Maintainability** | Pure transform functions (event bridge); dependency injection (function signatures as ports); shared contracts |
   225→| **Testability** | Pure core/effect shell pattern throughout; injectable clock/deps in stall detector; state reducer is pure |
   226→| **Simplicity** | Reuse existing SSE registry, event bridge, stall detector; no new infrastructure components |
   227→
   228→## 7. Architectural Constraints for Implementation
   229→
   230→These are critical wiring requirements the crafter must address:
   231→
   232→1. **SSE Registry must be passed to orchestrator wiring.** In `start-server.ts`, `wireOrchestratorRoutes` is currently called WITHOUT `sseRegistry`. Pass `deps.sse` as `sseRegistry` parameter. Without this, no events reach clients.
   233→2. **SSE stream must be registered before spawn.** Call `sseRegistry.registerMessage(streamId)` in `createOrchestratorSession` before starting the event iteration loop. The stream URL is returned to the client in the assign response -- clients connect immediately.
   234→3. **Orchestrator stream route must be added.** Register `/api/orchestrator/:workspaceId/sessions/:sessionId/stream` in `start-server.ts` route table. The handler delegates to `sseRegistry.handleStreamRequest(streamId)`.
   235→4. **Prompt endpoint inherits existing auth.** The POST /prompt endpoint uses the same Cookie-based auth as all orchestrator endpoints. No new authentication surface.
   236→5. **Event iteration must not block HTTP response.** The `for-await-of` loop is launched as an unlinked async IIFE. See ADR-006.
   237→6. **Token batching for log persistence.** Accumulate tokens in memory, flush at turn boundaries (idle status or user prompt). See ADR-005.
   238→
   239→## 8. Security
   240→
   241→- All orchestrator endpoints are behind existing Cookie-based authentication (extracted in `routes.ts` via `extractAuthToken`)
   242→- The prompt endpoint accepts only `{ text: string }` -- no injection surface beyond what `sendPrompt` already handles
   243→- SSE streams are scoped by `streamId` (UUID-based) -- not guessable
   244→- No new external network surface; OpenCode listens on `127.0.0.1` only
   245→
   246→## 9. Known Limitations
   247→
   248→- **Server restart loses handles**: In-memory `handleRegistry` is ephemeral. Active sessions become orphaned on restart. Documented limitation -- not addressed in this feature.
   249→- **No SSE reconnection for orchestrator streams**: If EventSource drops, client shows "Connection lost" but does not auto-reconnect. The useAgentSession hook closes on error. This matches the existing chat SSE behavior.
   250→- **Conversation log size**: Unbounded for now. Agent sessions are short-lived (minutes to hours). Cap at 10,000 entries if needed later.
   251→

    11→  session-lifecycle.ts    [MODIFY] -- add event iteration loop, prompt forwarding, log persistence calls
    12→  routes.ts               [MODIFY] -- add prompt endpoint, log endpoint
    13→  event-bridge.ts         [MODIFY] -- add log entry emission alongside SSE emission
    14→  spawn-opencode.ts       [NO CHANGE] -- already complete
    15→  stall-detector.ts       [NO CHANGE] -- already complete
    16→  conversation-log.ts     [NEW] -- conversation log CRUD (SurrealDB queries)
    17→  types.ts                [MODIFY] -- add ConversationLogEntry type if needed
    18→```

    20→### Streaming Domain (`app/src/server/streaming/`)

    23→

    25→streaming/

    32→

    34→runtime/

    45→```

    47→## Client-Side Boundaries
    48→
    49→### Agent Session UI (`app/src/client/components/graph/`)
    50→
    51→Owns: agent session visualization, interaction controls.
    52→
    53→```
    54→components/graph/
    55→  AgentStatusSection.tsx  [MODIFY] -- delegate to AgentSessionPanel when session active
    56→  AgentSessionPanel.tsx   [NEW] -- composite: output + prompt input + abort button
    57→  AgentSessionOutput.tsx  [NEW] -- token stream display with auto-scroll
    58→  AgentLogView.tsx        [NEW] -- chronological conversation log for review page

    78→
    79→## Boundary Rules
    80→
    81→1. **Orchestrator domain does NOT import from chat domain.** SSE registry is the shared integration point, injected via dependency.
    82→2. **Event bridge is the ONLY component that transforms OpenCode events.** All consumers receive `StreamEvent` types.
    83→3. **Handle registry is internal to session-lifecycle.** Routes access handles indirectly through lifecycle functions.
    84→4. **Conversation log queries are isolated in conversation-log.ts.** Session-lifecycle calls log functions but does not construct queries directly.
    85→5. **Client components consume `useAgentSession` hook state.** They do NOT directly interact with EventSource or SSE.

1→# Coding Session -- Data Models
     2→
     3→## Existing Schema (No Changes Required)
     4→
     5→The `agent_session` table already has all fields needed for session lifecycle:
     6→
     7→```
     8→agent_session (SCHEMAFULL)
     9→  agent: string
    10→  started_at: datetime
    11→  ended_at: option<datetime>
    12→  workspace: record<workspace>
    13→  task_id: option<record<task>>
    14→  orchestrator_status: option<string>  -- "spawning" | "active" | "idle" | "completed" | "aborted" | "error"
    15→  worktree_branch: option<string>
    16→  worktree_path: option<string>
    17→  stream_id: option<string>
    18→  opencode_session_id: option<string>
    19→  last_event_at: option<datetime>
    20→  last_feedback: option<string>
    21→  error_message: option<string>
    22→  files_changed: option<array<object>>
    23→  ...
    24→```
    25→
    26→## New Schema: Conversation Log
    27→
    28→New field on `agent_session` to store the conversation log inline. Simpler than a separate table -- each session has exactly one log, entries are always accessed together, and sessions are short-lived.
    29→
    30→### Schema Migration
    31→
    32→```
    33→DEFINE FIELD conversation_log ON agent_session TYPE option<array<object>>;
    34→DEFINE FIELD conversation_log[*].entry_type ON agent_session TYPE string
    35→  ASSERT $value IN ["agent_text", "file_change", "user_prompt", "status_change", "stall_warning"];
    36→DEFINE FIELD conversation_log[*].timestamp ON agent_session TYPE datetime;
    37→DEFINE FIELD conversation_log[*].content ON agent_session TYPE option<string>;
    38→DEFINE FIELD conversation_log[*].file ON agent_session TYPE option<string>;
    39→DEFINE FIELD conversation_log[*].change_type ON agent_session TYPE option<string>;
    40→DEFINE FIELD conversation_log[*].status ON agent_session TYPE option<string>;
    41→```
    42→
    43→### Log Entry Types
    44→
    45→| entry_type | content | file | change_type | status | When |
    46→|-----------|---------|------|-------------|--------|------|
    47→| `agent_text` | Token text (batched) | - | - | - | On `agent_token` events (batched per message turn) |
    48→| `file_change` | - | File path | created/modified/deleted | - | On `agent_file_change` event |
    49→| `user_prompt` | Prompt text | - | - | - | On POST /prompt or reject-with-feedback |
    50→| `status_change` | - | - | - | active/idle/completed/error/aborted | On `agent_status` event |
    51→| `stall_warning` | Warning message | - | - | - | On stall detection |
    52→
    53→### Token Batching Strategy
    54→
    55→`agent_token` events arrive as individual tokens (words/fragments). Appending each as a separate log entry would create thousands of entries. Instead:
    56→
    57→- Accumulate tokens in memory during a message turn
    58→- Flush as a single `agent_text` log entry when:
    59→  - A `status_change` to `idle` occurs (turn complete)
    60→  - A `user_prompt` is received (new turn boundary)
    61→  - A configurable flush interval elapses (30s safety net)
    62→
    63→This keeps the log at ~10-50 entries per session instead of thousands.
    64→
    65→## Shared Contract Types
    66→
    67→### AgentPromptEvent (new StreamEvent variant)
    68→
    69→```
    70→type: "agent_prompt"
    71→sessionId: string
    72→text: string
    73→```
    74→
    75→Emitted to SSE when a user sends a follow-up prompt. Allows the client to render user messages inline in the output stream without polling.
    76→
    77→### ConversationLogEntry (new shared type)
    78→
    79→```
    80→entryType: "agent_text" | "file_change" | "user_prompt" | "status_change" | "stall_warning"
    81→timestamp: string (ISO)
    82→content?: string
    83→file?: string
    84→changeType?: string
    85→status?: string
    86→```
    87→
    88→Used in the GET /log response and by the AgentLogView component.
    89→
    90→## Client-Side State
    91→
    92→### Extended AgentSessionState
    93→
    94→The `useAgentSession` hook state adds:
    95→
    96→- `tokens: Array<{ text: string; timestamp: string }>` -- accumulated token stream entries
    97→- `fileChanges: Array<{ file: string; changeType: string; timestamp: string }>` -- inline file change notifications
    98→- `userPrompts: Array<{ text: string; timestamp: string }>` -- echoed user prompts
    99→
   100→These are interleaved by timestamp in the AgentSessionOutput component for chronological display.
   101→
   102→## API Contracts
   103→
   104→### POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/prompt
   105→
   106→Request: `{ text: string }`
   107→Response: `202 Accepted` (empty body)
   108→Errors: 404 (session not found), 409 (terminal status)
   109→
   110→### GET /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/log
   111→
   112→Response: `{ entries: ConversationLogEntry[] }`
   113→Errors: 404 (session not found)
   114→
   115→Returns the persisted conversation log for the review page. The live session uses SSE events directly; this endpoint is for after-the-fact review.
   116→

1→# US-CS-001: Live Agent Output Stream
     2→
     3→## Problem
     4→Marcus Oliveira is a solo technical founder who delegates coding tasks to AI agents. After assigning a task, he currently sees only a status badge ("Working") with no visibility into what the agent is actually doing. He finds it frustrating to wait blindly for the agent to finish, unable to tell if it is making progress or heading in the wrong direction. His only option is to wait for idle status, then review a context-free diff.
     5→
     6→## Who
     7→- Solo technical founder | Assigns 3-5 coding tasks to AI agents daily | Needs real-time visibility into agent work to maintain trust and catch issues early
     8→
     9→## Solution
    10→Wire the existing event bridge to stream agent token output and file change notifications to the browser in real-time, rendering them in an output panel within the task entity detail view.
    11→
    12→## Job Trace
    13→- **J1**: When I have a well-defined task that I know an AI agent can handle, I want to assign it and trust that the agent understands what to build, so I can shift my attention to other work without worrying.
    14→- **Outcomes served**: #1 (minimize time to see agent begin), #2 (minimize likelihood of undetected wrong approach), #3 (minimize time to understand agent progress)
    15→
    16→## Domain Examples
    17→
    18→### 1: Happy Path -- Marcus watches the agent implement pagination
    19→Marcus assigns task "Add pagination to entity search" to an agent. Within 3 seconds, streaming text appears: "I'll add pagination to the entity search endpoint. Let me first look at the current implementation..." As the agent reads `entity-search-route.ts`, the output shows "Reading entity-search-route.ts..." When the agent saves a modified file, an inline notification appears: "entity-search-route.ts modified" and the files changed count increments to 1. Marcus can see the agent is taking the right approach.
    20→
    21→### 2: Edge Case -- Agent goes idle and output stops
    22→Marcus is watching the agent work on "Fix login redirect bug." After 2 minutes of active streaming, the output stops and the status badge changes from "Working" to "Idle." The "Review" link appears in the status section. The last output remains visible in the panel so Marcus can scroll back to understand what was done before reviewing the diff.
    23→
    24→### 3: Error/Stall -- No events for 30 seconds triggers stall warning
    25→Marcus assigned task "Refactor config loading" 4 minutes ago. The agent streamed output for 3 minutes, then stopped. After 30 seconds of silence, a warning appears: "Agent may be stalled (no activity for 30s)." The Abort button remains available. If no events arrive for 5 minutes total, the stall detector aborts the session automatically.
    26→
    27→## UAT Scenarios (BDD)
    28→
    29→### Scenario: Agent token events render as streaming text
    30→Given Marcus has an active agent session on task "Add pagination to entity search"
    31→And the event bridge is connected to the SSE registry
    32→When the agent emits a text response "I'll add pagination to the entity search endpoint"
    33→Then the text appears in the agent output panel within 200ms
    34→And the output panel auto-scrolls to the latest content
    35→
    36→### Scenario: File change events appear as inline notifications
    37→Given Marcus is watching the agent output stream for task "Add pagination to entity search"
    38→When the agent saves a modified file "entity-search-route.ts"
    39→Then an inline notification appears in the output: "entity-search-route.ts modified"
    40→And the "files changed" count in the status bar increments
    41→
    42→### Scenario: Status transitions update the badge in real-time
    43→Given Marcus has an agent session with status "spawning"
    44→When the agent begins responding
    45→Then the status badge changes from "Spawning" to "Working"
    46→And when the agent becomes idle
    47→Then the status badge changes from "Working" to "Idle"
    48→And a "Review" link appears
    49→
    50→### Scenario: Stall warning appears after 30 seconds of inactivity
    51→Given Marcus has an active agent session that has been streaming for 3 minutes
    52→When no events are received for 30 seconds
    53→Then a warning message appears "Agent may be stalled (no activity for 30s)"
    54→And the "Abort" button remains available
    55→
    56→### Scenario: Output persists when scrolling back during active stream
    57→Given Marcus is watching agent output that exceeds the visible panel height
    58→When Marcus scrolls up to review earlier output
    59→Then the earlier output is visible and readable
    60→And new output continues accumulating below the visible area
    61→
    62→## Acceptance Criteria
    63→- [ ] Agent token events stream into an output panel in the entity detail view
    64→- [ ] Output auto-scrolls to latest content during active streaming
    65→- [ ] File change events appear as distinct inline notifications within the output
    66→- [ ] Files changed count updates in the status bar from file change events
    67→- [ ] Status badge updates in real-time from status events (spawning -> active -> idle -> completed)
    68→- [ ] Stall warning appears after 30 seconds of no events
    69→- [ ] Output remains scrollable and readable when agent is idle or completed
    70→
    71→## Technical Notes
    72→- Event bridge (`event-bridge.ts`) and stall detector (`stall-detector.ts`) exist but are not wired to `createOrchestratorSession`
    73→- `useAgentSession` hook already handles `agent_status`, `agent_file_change`, and `agent_stall_warning` events but does not handle `agent_token` events
    74→- New component `AgentSessionOutput` needed to accumulate and render token text
    75→- SSE event types: `agent_token` (streaming text), `agent_file_change` (file notifications), `agent_status` (status transitions)
    76→- Auto-scroll must not fight user scroll-up (pause auto-scroll when user scrolls away from bottom)
    77→
    78→## Dependencies
    79→- Depends on: spawn-opencode.ts returning eventStream (exists, see `spawn-opencode.ts` lines 181-204)
    80→- Depends on: event-bridge.ts transformOpencodeEvent (exists, see `event-bridge.ts` lines 64-98)
    81→- Depends on: SSE registry infrastructure (exists)
    82→- Blocked by: Nothing -- all infrastructure exists, needs wiring
    83→

1→# US-CS-002: Follow-Up Prompt to Running Agent
     2→
     3→## Problem
     4→Marcus Oliveira is a solo technical founder who delegates coding tasks to AI agents. When he notices the agent heading in the wrong direction (e.g., writing a new utility function when one already exists), his only option is to abort the entire session and lose all progress. He finds it wasteful and frustrating to have no middle ground between "let it run" and "kill it." He needs a way to send guidance to the agent without destroying its work.
     5→
     6→## Who
     7→- Solo technical founder | Watches agent output in real-time | Needs to course-correct without losing progress when the agent takes a wrong approach
     8→
     9→## Solution
    10→Add a text input below the agent output panel for sending follow-up prompts to active sessions, backed by a new POST endpoint that delivers messages via the registered sendPrompt handle.
    11→
    12→## Job Trace
    13→- **J2**: When I notice the agent is heading in the wrong direction, I want to send it guidance without disrupting its flow, so I can steer it toward the right solution without starting over.
    14→- **Outcomes served**: #4 (minimize time to course-correct), #5 (minimize likelihood of losing progress), #6 (minimize anxiety about prompt disruption)
    15→
    16→## Domain Examples
    17→
    18→### 1: Happy Path -- Marcus redirects the agent to use an existing function
    19→Marcus is watching the agent work on "Add pagination to entity search." The output shows "I'll create a new pagination helper function in utils/pagination.ts..." Marcus types "Use the existing paginate() function from app/src/shared/query-helpers.ts instead of writing a new one" and clicks Send. His message appears in the output as a visually distinct block. The agent responds "Good catch! I'll use the existing paginate() function" and continues working with the corrected approach.
    20→
    21→### 2: Edge Case -- Marcus sends additional instructions to an idle agent
    22→The agent finished its initial work on "Add pagination to entity search" and went idle. Marcus reviews the output and realizes cursor-based pagination would also be useful. He types "Also add cursor-based pagination as an alternative to offset pagination" and sends. The session transitions from "idle" to "active" and the agent begins implementing the additional feature.
    23→
    24→### 3: Error/Boundary -- Prompt rejected for a terminal session
    25→The agent session on "Fix login redirect" has been aborted. Marcus navigates back to the task detail. The message input is disabled with a note "Session was aborted." Marcus must assign a new agent session if he wants to continue the work.
    26→
    27→## UAT Scenarios (BDD)
    28→
    29→### Scenario: Marcus sends a follow-up prompt to a working agent
    30→Given Marcus has an active agent session on task "Add pagination to entity search"
    31→And the agent output shows "I'll create a new pagination helper function"
    32→When Marcus types "Use the existing paginate() function from query-helpers.ts instead" in the message input
    33→And clicks "Send"
    34→Then the endpoint responds with 202 Accepted
    35→And Marcus's message appears in the output as a distinct user message block
    36→And the input field clears and re-enables for the next message
    37→And the agent responds acknowledging the guidance within 5 seconds
    38→
    39→### Scenario: Marcus sends a prompt to an idle agent
    40→Given the agent session on task "Add pagination to entity search" has status "idle"
    41→When Marcus types "Also add cursor-based pagination as an alternative" and clicks "Send"
    42→Then the session status transitions from "idle" to "active"
    43→And the agent begins working on the additional request
    44→And the output continues in the same panel
    45→
    46→### Scenario: Prompt input is disabled for completed session
    47→Given the agent session on task "Add pagination to entity search" has status "completed"
    48→When Marcus views the session panel
    49→Then the message input is disabled
    50→And a note below the input explains "Session has ended."
    51→
    52→### Scenario: Prompt input is disabled for aborted session
    53→Given the agent session on task "Fix login redirect" has status "aborted"
    54→When Marcus views the session panel
    55→Then the message input is disabled
    56→And a note below the input explains "Session was aborted."
    57→
    58→### Scenario: Prompt delivery fails gracefully when handle is missing
    59→Given the agent session "sess-12345" was active but the server restarted
    60→And the sendPrompt handle is no longer in the in-memory registry
    61→When Marcus sends a follow-up prompt
    62→Then the endpoint responds with 404
    63→And an error message shows "Session not found. The agent may have been restarted."
    64→
    65→## Acceptance Criteria
    66→- [ ] A text input with Send button appears below the agent output when session is active or idle
    67→- [ ] Submitting a prompt delivers the text to the agent via POST endpoint (202 response)
    68→- [ ] User messages appear in the output as visually distinct blocks (different from agent text)
    69→- [ ] Input clears and re-enables after successful send
    70→- [ ] Input is disabled when session status is completed, aborted, or error
    71→- [ ] Disabled input shows an explanatory note for the terminal state
    72→- [ ] Sending a prompt to an idle session transitions it back to active
    73→
    74→## Technical Notes
    75→- POST endpoint: `/api/orchestrator/:workspaceId/sessions/:sessionId/prompt`
    76→- Request body: `{ text: string }`
    77→- Response: 202 Accepted (fire-and-forget delivery)
    78→- Error responses: 404 if session/handle not found, 409 if session in terminal status
    79→- sendPrompt handle stored in handleRegistry (in-memory Map in session-lifecycle.ts)
    80→- Handle loss on server restart is a known limitation -- sessions are not resumable after restart
    81→- Client API wrapper needed in `orchestrator-api.ts`
    82→
    83→## Dependencies
    84→- Depends on: US-CS-001 (live output stream -- user needs to see agent output to know when to intervene)
    85→- Depends on: sendPrompt function on OpenCodeHandle (exists, see `spawn-opencode.ts` lines 194-199)
    86→- Depends on: handleRegistry in session-lifecycle.ts (exists, see line 100)
    87→

1→# US-CS-003: Wire Event Stream into Session Lifecycle
     2→
     3→## Problem
     4→Marcus Oliveira is a solo technical founder who delegates coding tasks to AI agents. The orchestrator can spawn an agent and the event bridge can transform OpenCode events into Brain stream events, but these two pieces are not connected. After assignment, no events flow to the browser because `createOrchestratorSession` does not iterate the spawn handle's `eventStream` or pipe events through the event bridge. Marcus sees only a status badge with no live feedback.
     5→
     6→## Who
     7→- Solo technical founder | Assigns tasks to AI agents | Infrastructure gap prevents him from seeing any live agent activity despite all components existing
     8→
     9→## Solution
    10→After spawn succeeds in `createOrchestratorSession`, iterate `handle.eventStream`, pipe each event through `startEventBridge` to the SSE registry, and start the stall detector to monitor for stalled sessions.
    11→
    12→## Job Trace
    13→- **J1**: Delegate coding work with confidence -- this is the enabling infrastructure that makes live output (US-CS-001) and follow-up prompts (US-CS-002) possible.
    14→- **Outcomes served**: #1 (minimize time between assigning and seeing agent begin), #2 (minimize undetected wrong approach)
    15→
    16→## Domain Examples
    17→
    18→### 1: Happy Path -- Events flow from OpenCode to browser
    19→Marcus assigns task "Add pagination to entity search." The spawn succeeds and returns an eventStream. The session lifecycle starts iterating the stream. When the agent emits a `message.part.updated` event, the event bridge transforms it to an `agent_token` StreamEvent and emits it to the SSE registry. The browser's EventSource receives it within 200ms.
    20→
    21→### 2: Edge Case -- Agent session ends and event iteration stops
    22→The agent working on "Fix login redirect" finishes and emits a `session.updated` event with status "completed." The event bridge transforms it to an `agent_status` event. The session lifecycle detects the terminal status, stops iterating the event stream, and stops the stall detector. The SSE stream remains open for the client to receive the final status event.
    23→
    24→### 3: Error/Boundary -- Event stream errors during iteration
    25→The agent working on "Refactor config loading" crashes mid-task. The event stream throws an error during iteration. The session lifecycle catches the error, updates the session status to "error," emits an `agent_status` error event to the SSE stream, and stops the stall detector.
    26→
    27→## UAT Scenarios (BDD)
    28→
    29→### Scenario: OpenCode events flow through event bridge to SSE registry
    30→Given Marcus has assigned an agent to task "Add pagination to entity search"
    31→And the spawn returned a valid eventStream and sessionId
    32→When the agent emits a text response event
    33→Then the event bridge transforms it to an `agent_token` StreamEvent
    34→And the SSE registry emits it on the session's stream
    35→And the browser receives the token event
    36→
    37→### Scenario: Stall detector starts monitoring after spawn
    38→Given Marcus has assigned an agent to task "Add pagination to entity search"
    39→And the spawn completed successfully
    40→When the session lifecycle wires the event bridge
    41→Then the stall detector starts monitoring with the configured timeout (5 minutes) and step limit (100)
    42→And file change events increment the stall detector's step count
    43→And any event resets the stall detector's activity timer
    44→
    45→### Scenario: Event iteration stops on terminal session status
    46→Given the agent is working on task "Fix login redirect"
    47→When the agent emits a session.updated event with status "completed"
    48→Then the event bridge emits the final agent_status event
    49→And event stream iteration stops
    50→And the stall detector is stopped
    51→
    52→### Scenario: Event stream error updates session status
    53→Given the agent is working on task "Refactor config loading"
    54→When the event stream throws an error during iteration
    55→Then the session status is updated to "error" in the database
    56→And an agent_status error event is emitted to the SSE stream
    57→And the stall detector is stopped
    58→
    59→## Acceptance Criteria
    60→- [ ] `createOrchestratorSession` iterates `handle.eventStream` after successful spawn
    61→- [ ] Each event is piped through `startEventBridge` which transforms and emits to SSE registry
    62→- [ ] Stall detector starts monitoring after event bridge is wired
    63→- [ ] Event iteration stops on terminal status events (completed, aborted, error)
    64→- [ ] Event stream errors are caught, session status updated to "error," and error event emitted
    65→- [ ] Session status transitions to "active" after the first agent event is received
    66→
    67→## Technical Notes
    68→- This is a Technical Task that enables US-CS-001 and US-CS-002
    69→- `startEventBridge` exists in `event-bridge.ts` -- takes deps, streamId, sessionId, optional stallDetector
    70→- `startStallDetector` exists in `stall-detector.ts` -- takes deps, config, sessionId, streamId
    71→- Event iteration should be fire-and-forget (not awaited in createOrchestratorSession) to avoid blocking the HTTP response
    72→- The async iteration loop should run in the background after the session creation response is returned
    73→- `updateLastEventAt` dependency: needs a function that merges `{ last_event_at: new Date() }` to the session record
    74→
    75→## Dependencies
    76→- Depends on: spawn-opencode.ts eventStream (exists)
    77→- Depends on: event-bridge.ts startEventBridge (exists)
    78→- Depends on: stall-detector.ts startStallDetector (exists)
    79→- Depends on: SSE registry emitEvent (exists)
    80→- Blocked by: Nothing -- all components exist, needs orchestration in session-lifecycle.ts
    81→

1→# US-CS-004: Contextual Review with Agent Conversation Log
     2→
     3→## Problem
     4→Marcus Oliveira is a solo technical founder who reviews AI agent code changes before accepting them. The current review page shows only a raw diff with no context about how or why the agent made each change. For complex tasks, Marcus must mentally reconstruct the agent's reasoning from the diff alone, which is nearly as much work as doing the task himself. When he sent follow-up prompts to course-correct the agent, none of that context is visible in the review.
     5→
     6→## Who
     7→- Solo technical founder | Reviews 3-5 agent diffs daily | Needs to understand the reasoning behind each change to make confident accept/reject decisions without re-reading all the code
     8→
     9→## Solution
    10→Add an "Agent Log" tab to the review page that shows the full conversation trail (agent output + user prompts) alongside the existing diff view.
    11→
    12→## Job Trace
    13→- **J3**: When the agent has finished and I need to decide whether to accept its changes, I want to review the diff alongside the agent's reasoning, so I can make a confident accept/reject decision.
    14→- **Outcomes served**: #7 (minimize time to understand why each change was made), #8 (minimize undetected issues), #9 (minimize review-reject cycles)
    15→
    16→## Domain Examples
    17→
    18→### 1: Happy Path -- Marcus reviews a course-corrected session
    19→Marcus reviews the diff for "Add pagination to entity search." The Diff tab shows 3 files changed. He switches to the Agent Log tab and sees: (1) the agent's plan to create a new helper, (2) his course correction "Use the existing paginate() function," (3) the agent's acknowledgment and revised approach, (4) file change notifications. With this context, Marcus immediately understands why `query-helpers.ts` is imported instead of a new `utils/pagination.ts` being created. Review takes 2 minutes instead of 10.
    20→
    21→### 2: Edge Case -- Session with no user prompts
    22→Marcus reviews the diff for "Fix typo in error message." The agent ran without intervention. The Agent Log tab shows a short conversation: the agent read the file, identified the typo, fixed it. One file changed. The log confirms the fix is straightforward and Marcus accepts in 30 seconds.
    23→
    24→### 3: Error/Boundary -- Reject with feedback references specific log context
    25→Marcus reviews "Refactor config loading." The Agent Log shows the agent skipped updating tests. Marcus clicks "Reject with Feedback" and writes "You updated config-loader.ts but didn't update the corresponding test in config-loader.test.ts. Please update the test to match." The feedback references what Marcus learned from the log, not just the diff.
    26→
    27→## UAT Scenarios (BDD)
    28→
    29→### Scenario: Review page shows Agent Log tab alongside Diff tab
    30→Given the agent has completed work on task "Add pagination to entity search"
    31→And the session included Marcus's course correction about using existing paginate()
    32→When Marcus navigates to the review page
    33→Then a "Diff" tab shows the unified diff (default active)
    34→And an "Agent Log" tab shows the full conversation trail
    35→And the Agent Log includes the agent's output, Marcus's messages, and file change notifications
    36→
    37→### Scenario: Agent Log shows user prompts as distinct entries
    38→Given Marcus sent a follow-up prompt "Use the existing paginate() function" during the session
    39→When Marcus views the Agent Log tab on the review page
    40→Then Marcus's message appears as a visually distinct entry (different from agent text)
    41→And the chronological order is preserved (agent output, user prompt, agent response)
    42→
    43→### Scenario: Review page for session with no user intervention
    44→Given the agent completed task "Fix typo in error message" without any user prompts
    45→When Marcus navigates to the review page
    46→Then the Agent Log tab shows only agent output and file change notifications
    47→And no user message entries appear
    48→
    49→### Scenario: Reject feedback informed by agent log context
    50→Given Marcus is reviewing task "Refactor config loading"
    51→And the Agent Log shows the agent modified config-loader.ts but not config-loader.test.ts
    52→When Marcus clicks "Reject with Feedback"
    53→Then a textarea appears for entering feedback
    54→And Marcus can type specific feedback referencing what the log revealed
    55→And clicking "Send Feedback & Resume Agent" delivers the feedback and returns to live view
    56→
    57→### Scenario: File change notifications in log link to diff sections
    58→Given the Agent Log shows "entity-search-route.ts modified"
    59→When Marcus clicks on the file change notification in the log
    60→Then the view switches to the Diff tab
    61→And scrolls to the diff section for "entity-search-route.ts"
    62→
    63→## Acceptance Criteria
    64→- [ ] Review page has two tabs: "Diff" (default) and "Agent Log"
    65→- [ ] Agent Log displays the full chronological conversation trail from the session
    66→- [ ] User prompts are visually distinct from agent output in the log
    67→- [ ] File change notifications appear inline in the log with file names
    68→- [ ] Session metadata (duration, files changed count) is shown in the review header
    69→- [ ] Reject flow includes a feedback textarea and "Send Feedback & Resume Agent" button
    70→- [ ] Rejecting navigates back to the live session view with the agent working on the feedback
    71→
    72→## Technical Notes
    73→- Agent conversation log source: TBD -- either server-side persistence of token events + user prompts, or client-side accumulation passed to review page
    74→- If server-side: needs new field or table to store accumulated agent output per session
    75→- If client-side: log data must be passed via URL state or session storage to the review page route
    76→- Server-side persistence is recommended because it survives page navigation and enables review of sessions where the user navigated away during agent work
    77→- Existing review page (`review-page.tsx`) fetches session review data via `getSessionReview` -- needs to include conversation log
    78→- Existing review response type (`SessionReviewResponse`) needs a conversationLog field
    79→
    80→## Dependencies
    81→- Depends on: US-CS-001 (live output stream must accumulate tokens for the log)
    82→- Depends on: US-CS-002 (follow-up prompts must be recorded in the log)
    83→- Depends on: US-CS-003 (event stream wiring provides the event flow)
    84→- Depends on: Existing review page and accept/reject flow (exists)
    85→

   141→**CM-A (Driving port usage)**: All test files import from `coding-session-test-kit.ts` which re-exports from `orchestrator-test-kit.ts`. Tests invoke through HTTP endpoints only. Zero internal component imports.
   142→
   143→**CM-B (Business language purity)**: Gherkin uses domain terms: "Marcus", "coding agent", "follow-up prompt", "conversation log", "session", "review". Zero technical terms (no HTTP, JSON, API, database, SSE in Gherkin).
   144→
   145→**CM-C (Walking skeleton + focused counts)**: 3 walking skeletons + 28 focused scenarios = 31 total. Error/edge ratio: 52%.
   146→

1→# Walking Skeleton Design Rationale
     2→
     3→## Skeletons Selected
     4→
     5→### Skeleton 1: Assign, Observe, Send Follow-Up
     6→**Traces**: US-CS-003 + US-CS-001 + US-CS-002
     7→
     8→The most critical E2E path: Marcus assigns a task, the event stream wires up and delivers agent output, and Marcus sends a follow-up prompt. This exercises:
     9→- Session creation (existing) triggering event stream iteration (new)
    10→- Event bridge forwarding to SSE registry (new wiring)
    11→- Prompt endpoint delivering instructions to running agent (new)
    12→
    13→**Litmus test**: A stakeholder can confirm "yes, an engineering lead needs to see what the agent is doing and tell it to adjust course."
    14→
    15→### Skeleton 2: Review Conversation Log
    16→**Traces**: US-CS-003 + US-CS-004
    17→
    18→After the agent completes, Marcus opens the review and sees the full conversation log. This exercises:
    19→- Conversation log persistence during event processing (new)
    20→- Log retrieval endpoint (new)
    21→- Review page integration with log data (new)
    22→
    23→**Litmus test**: A stakeholder can confirm "yes, the reviewer needs the agent's reasoning trail to make an informed accept/reject decision."
    24→
    25→### Skeleton 3: Agent Error Notification
    26→**Traces**: US-CS-001 + US-CS-003
    27→
    28→When the agent encounters an error, the session stops and Marcus is notified through the event stream. This exercises:
    29→- Error event forwarding through event bridge (existing transform, new wiring)
    30→- Session status update on stream error (new)
    31→- Error event delivery to client (new wiring)
    32→
    33→**Litmus test**: A stakeholder can confirm "yes, if the agent crashes, the lead needs to know immediately."
    34→
    35→## Why These Three
    36→
    37→1. **Skeleton 1** proves the interactive supervision loop works end-to-end (the core value proposition).
    38→2. **Skeleton 2** proves the review experience has contextual information (the informed decision value).
    39→3. **Skeleton 3** proves failure visibility (the reliability value).
    40→
    41→Together they cover: event stream wiring (US-CS-003), live output (US-CS-001), follow-up prompts (US-CS-002), and contextual review (US-CS-004).
    42→
    43→## Implementation Sequence
    44→
    45→1. Skeleton 1 (enabled first) -- drives event stream wiring + prompt endpoint
    46→2. Skeleton 3 (enable second) -- drives error handling in event iteration
    47→3. Skeleton 2 (enable third) -- drives conversation log persistence + retrieval
    48→

    23→      - 'OpenCodeHandle exposes sendPrompt to deliver follow-up text to the session'
    24→      - 'OpenCodeHandle exposes async-iterable event stream from client.event.subscribe()'

    28→    implementation_scope:

    30→      - app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts
    31→  - id: 01-02
    32→    name: 'Wire event stream into session lifecycle'
    33→    description: >
    34→      After spawn succeeds in createOrchestratorSession, iterate
    35→      handle.eventStream, pipe each event through startEventBridge to
    36→      the SSE registry, and start the stall detector.
    37→    criteria:

    39→      - 'Stall detector starts monitoring activity on the new session'
    40→      - 'Session status transitions to active after first agent event received'
    41→      - 'Event iteration stops on session abort or terminal status'

    53→  steps:

    62→      - 'Endpoint returns 404 if session not found, 409 if session is in terminal status'

    64→      - 'Handle registry stores sendPrompt alongside abort for each active session'

    66→    dependencies:
    67→      - 01-01
    68→      - 01-02

    70→      - app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts
    71→      - app/src/server/orchestrator/routes.ts
    72→  - id: 02-02
    73→    name: 'Client API wrapper for follow-up prompts'
    74→    description: >
    75→      Add sendPrompt function to orchestrator-api.ts client module
    76→      and corresponding shared contract type for the prompt request.
    77→    criteria:
    78→      - 'Client function sends POST to prompt endpoint with text payload'
    79→      - 'Error handling follows existing orchestratorFetch pattern (throw on non-OK)'

<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : coding-session -->
<!-- DES-STEP-ID : 01-01 -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: 01-01
Project: coding-session
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/nw-functional-software-crafter/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md
Load on-demand per phase as specified in your Skill Loading Strategy table.

# TASK_CONTEXT
Step: 01-01
Name: Enrich OpenCodeHandle with prompt and event stream
Description: Extend OpenCodeHandle to carry sendPrompt and async-iterable eventStream. Initial prompt is /brain-start-task <taskId> which triggers the brain CLI to load task context and validate CLI installation + authentication. If brain CLI is missing or not authenticated, OpenCode surfaces the error.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Initial prompt delivers task context to agent for parsing and validation
- OpenCodeHandle exposes sendPrompt to deliver follow-up text to the session
- OpenCodeHandle exposes async-iterable event stream from client.event.subscribe()
- Abort cleans up event subscription, session, and server process

Files to modify:
- app/src/server/orchestrator/spawn-opencode.ts
- app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts

Test directory: tests/unit/orchestrator/

Dependencies: none
Time estimate: 2h

IMPORTANT CONTEXT:
- Read the existing files before modifying: app/src/server/orchestrator/spawn-opencode.ts, app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts
- Read the design docs for architectural context: docs/feature/coding-session/design/architecture-design.md, docs/feature/coding-session/design/component-boundaries.md, docs/feature/coding-session/design/data-models.md
- Read test scenarios: docs/feature/coding-session/distill/test-scenarios.md (find scenarios relevant to step 01-01)
- Read the existing test file: tests/unit/coding-agent-orchestrator/session-lifecycle.test.ts for testing patterns
- Follow the project's CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md conventions (functional paradigm, no null, etc.)
- This is a TypeScript/Bun project

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Load context, verify prerequisites, read existing source files and design docs
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Write failing acceptance test
2. RED_UNIT - Write failing unit test
3. GREEN - Minimal code to pass tests
   After GREEN: run FULL test suite. If all pass, proceed to COMMIT immediately.
   Never move to new task or stop without committing green code.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with conventional message
   Include git trailer: `Step-ID: 01-01` (required for DES verification)
   Example:
   ```
   feat(coding-session): enrich OpenCodeHandle with prompt and event stream

   Step-ID: 01-01
   ```

# QUALITY_GATES
- All tests pass before COMMIT
- No skipped phases without blocked_by reason
- Coverage maintained or improved

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/coding-session \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases (genuinely not applicable):

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/coding-session \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

CLI enforces real UTC timestamps and validates phase names.
Do NOT manually edit execution-log.yaml.

CRITICAL: Only the executing agent calls the CLI.

# RECORDING_INTEGRITY
- Phase logging is mandatory after each TDD phase completes
- Use the DES CLI commands exactly as specified in OUTCOME_RECORDING
- Never fabricate, backdate, or skip phase log entries
- If a phase is genuinely not applicable, use SKIPPED status with reason
- The execution-log.yaml is append-only; never edit or delete existing entries
- DES hooks verify phase entries match actual work performed

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only modify files listed in step's files_to_modify (app/src/server/orchestrator/spawn-opencode.ts, app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts) plus test files
- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Do not modify execution-log.yaml structure (append only via CLI)
- NEVER write execution-log entries for phases you did not execute

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.
If GREEN complete (all tests pass), MUST commit before returning — even at turn limit.

TASK BOUNDARY: Complete step 01-01 only. Return control to orchestrator after completion.
